raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The section draws strong conclusions ('substantially reduces', 'survey-agnostic strategy') from modest global metric deltas (0.8–0.9 pp accuracy); reviewing requires checking whether those inferential steps are logically warranted.","R1.3":"The opening paragraph asserts '~35% reduction in magnitude-dependent bias', but the reported metrics (accuracy, macro-F1, ROC-AUC over entire test splits) are aggregate, not bias-stratified — the direct evidential link to the 35% figure is absent in this section and must be audited.","R1.6":"Bare 'review' delegation plausibly includes sentence and paragraph quality; weight is elevated but not load-bearing pending clarification on whether scientific or stylistic review is primary."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review primarily check scientific content — verifying that claims (especially the '~35% bias reduction') are logically and evidentially grounded in the reported results — or is the goal a writing/style review focused on clarity, grammar, and prose quality?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section draws strong conclusions ('substantially reduces', 'survey-agnostic strategy') from modest global metric deltas (0.8–0.9 pp accuracy); reviewing requires checking whether those inferential steps are logically warranted.
rationale.R1.3: The opening paragraph asserts '~35% reduction in magnitude-dependent bias', but the reported metrics (accuracy, macro-F1, ROC-AUC over entire test splits) are aggregate, not bias-stratified — the direct evidential link to the 35% figure is absent in this section and must be audited.
rationale.R1.6: Bare 'review' delegation plausibly includes sentence and paragraph quality; weight is elevated but not load-bearing pending clarification on whether scientific or stylistic review is primary.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review primarily check scientific content — verifying that claims (especially the '~35% bias reduction') are logically and evidentially grounded in the reported results — or is the goal a writing/style review focused on clarity, grammar, and prose quality?
